herbs.herb_desc: Acorus calamus decoction, also known as Indian sarsaparilla or vacha, is a traditional herbal remedy prepared from the roots of the Acorus calamus plant. The decoction is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and reduce stress. Traditionally consumed as a tea or added to food, Acorus calamus decoction is used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall well-being and balance.

Alpinia officinarum decoction, also known as galangal decoction, is a traditional herbal remedy derived from the rhizomes of the Alpinia officinarum plant. The decoction has been used in various cultures, particularly in Southeast Asia and China, to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation. Its therapeutic properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.
